agent_reply: - `BtManager` namespace with the requested state machine.
- Connect by MAC, with a bonded-device check that returns `NotBonded`.
- Pairing mode and incoming-connection waiting mode.
- Disconnect that also ends pairing/waiting/connection attempts.
- Pairing success callback with MAC + name, then immediate disconnect while in pairing mode.
- Audio callback registration for incoming/outgoing HFP audio.
- I added a temporary compile harness so PlatformIO would actually build `lib/BluetoothManager`, and that passed.
- I removed the harness afterward and ran the normal `pio run`; that also passed.
- One caveat: because nothing in `src` currently includes `BluetoothManager.h`, PlatformIO won’t link it into the final firmware until a real module starts using it.

Strategy note: your plan makes sense overall. The only part I’d want to test on hardware is “waiting for incoming connection”, because HFP client/server roles and phone behavior can be picky there. The implementation matches the existing pairing demo style: make the ESP32 connectable/discoverable, then let the HFP callbacks drive state.

user_prompt: to `BtHostList`, add a method to insert a new entry, and have BluetoothManager call it when pairing is successful
agent_reply: bool insert(const char* name, const esp_bd_addr_t bdaddr);
- Appends a new host if the MAC is not already in the list.
- If the MAC already exists, it refreshes `bonded`.
- It preserves an existing non-empty name, so a JSON-edited friendly name stays authoritative.
- If the existing name is empty and pairing provides one, it fills it in.
- Allocation failure is detectable via `false` and `lastResult()`.
`BluetoothManager` now owns a singleton host list:
Build succeeded. Note: `BtHostList.h/.cpp` are still untracked new files, so remember to add them when committing.

user_prompt: I need both a new enum item for when audio is available, and a callback event that is fired to the existing `StateChangedCallback`
agent_reply: and `StateChangedCallback` will receive it when HFP audio routing actually comes up, from either CVSD or mSBC audio state events.
I also made audio disconnect transition back to `Connected`, so the sequence should now look like:
Connecting -> Connected -> Audio Available -> Connected -> Idle/Reconnecting

I updated internal Bluetooth checks so `Connected` and `AudioAvailable` both count as an HFP control connection where appropriate, while `canNotifyOutgoingAudioReady()` still requires real audio availability. The speakerphone test now treats both states as connected for controls.

user_prompt: ok, I want to make a general usage flow change. the device will always be connectable but not discoverable, pairing mode will make it discoverable. Activating Wi-Fi will shut everything down. There will no longer need to be a waiting mode in addition to pairing mode.
I don't see this mentioned in my design brainstorm documents, if I did and I missed it, warn me
agent_reply: I did find the old idea in two places:
I updated those. The only remaining “wait for incoming connection” text is in [ui-design.md](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/ui-design.md), but that is about Wi-Fi AP mode, so I left it alone.
- Bluetooth now initializes as connectable but non-discoverable.
- Pairing mode makes it discoverable temporarily.
- Successful pairing closes discovery and returns to connectable/non-discoverable.
- Wi-Fi station/AP/scan activation now stops audio, stops recording, and shuts down Bluetooth first.

user_prompt: ok then implement automatic reconnection, attempts are every 2 seconds (unless you know if there's an internal timeout that we can follow instead)
put `BtManager::poll()` before `AudioManager::pump_task();` unless you have reason to believe it needs to live in the other thread
agent_reply: - On unexpected disconnect, `BtManager` now remembers the last peer MAC and enters `Reconnecting`.
- If the stack accepts a reconnect attempt, state moves to `Connecting` and waits for Bluedroid’s connection result before trying again.
- User-requested `disconnect()`, `shutdown()`, and pairing mode clear the reconnect schedule so they don’t fight the user.

agent_reply: The log still shows the phone closing HFP before we ever reach `CONNECTED` or `SLC_CONNECTED`, so the ESP32 API call is starting, but Android is rejecting the profile connection.
I made the main firmware match the old working test path more closely:

- [src/main.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:116) now generates/logs the legacy PIN and calls `BtManager::init(nullptr, ..., generatedLegacyPin())`, so the device name should become `The Fly C6A6` instead of plain `The Fly`.
- [BluetoothManager.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BluetoothManager.h:48) now defaults `deviceName` to `nullptr`, meaning generated names are the default path.
- [BluetoothManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BluetoothManager.cpp:334) now explicitly configures EIR data to include local name, TX power, and UUIDs.
- [BluetoothManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BluetoothManager.cpp:1080) now logs `ESP_HF_CLIENT_PROF_STATE_EVT` instead of calling it unknown.
- Added GAP logs for EIR config and ACL connect/disconnect events.

For the next test, Android should forget the old `"The Fly"` bond before pairing again. Same BDADDR plus stale Android profile state can absolutely preserve the bad “Calls off / address-only” behavior across firmware changes. The new boot log should show `device_name="The Fly C6A6"` and `hfp profile state: ESP_HF_INIT_SUCCESS`.

I made the main firmware match the old working test path more closely:

- [src/main.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:116) now generates/logs the legacy PIN and calls `BtManager::init(nullptr, ..., generatedLegacyPin())`, so the device name should become `The Fly C6A6` instead of plain `The Fly`.
- [BluetoothManager.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BluetoothManager.h:48) now defaults `deviceName` to `nullptr`, meaning generated names are the default path.
- [BluetoothManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BluetoothManager.cpp:334) now explicitly configures EIR data to include local name, TX power, and UUIDs.
- [BluetoothManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BluetoothManager.cpp:1080) now logs `ESP_HF_CLIENT_PROF_STATE_EVT` instead of calling it unknown.
- Added GAP logs for EIR config and ACL connect/disconnect events.

For the next test, Android should forget the old `"The Fly"` bond before pairing again. Same BDADDR plus stale Android profile state can absolutely preserve the bad “Calls off / address-only” behavior across firmware changes. The new boot log should show `device_name="The Fly C6A6"` and `hfp profile state: ESP_HF_INIT_SUCCESS`.

commit_message: progress on bluetooth but cannot initiate connection, can handle incoming though

user_prompt: the crash was upon completion of pairing
agent_reply: Yep, that explains the stack traces. Pairing completion was letting the phone immediately kick off HFP service setup, which is the fragile Bluedroid path.
I changed it so boot/pairing use Bluetooth GAP only, and HFP is initialized lazily when you actually connect to a saved host:
- Changed `setup()` in [main.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp) to use GAP-only init, while still storing audio callbacks.
- Changed `startPairing()` in [BluetoothManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BluetoothManager.cpp) to avoid HFP during pairing, and deinit HFP before pairing if it was already up.
- Left the internal HFP debug flags disabled in [platformio.ini](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini), since they’re still too close to the WDT path for comfort.
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec0`, build passes.

user_prompt: 3. request HFP audio/control disconnect (actual disconnected event arrives asynchronously)
6. on dismiss, set BT connectable and non-discoverable
agent_reply: 4. stops audio and drains/flushes/closes the recording file
6. dialog dismiss callback restores BT to connectable/non-discoverable

I added `BtManager::disconnectNonConnectable()` in [BluetoothManager.h](/c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BluetoothManager.h) / [BluetoothManager.cpp](/c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BluetoothManager.cpp), while leaving normal `disconnect()` behavior unchanged. The async disconnected event now respects a “hold non-connectable” latch so it won’t reopen connectability before the modal is dismissed.
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec0`; build passes.

commit_message: prevent rare race condition that restarts a recording from phone
